calendar.matters: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution Or A Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jul-15-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Randall Wilson for Kevin Richards, Petitioner (via Zoom).

Mr. Wilson explained that the original will could not be located despite diligent searches by the decedent's sister, Kathleen Hambly, and the decedent's financial advisor, Henry Korngut. Both declarants confirmed they did not believe the decedent intended to revoke the will. Mr. Wilson argued that the evidence overcame the presumption of revocation under Probate Code Section 6124.

Mr. Wilson further addressed the nomination of Kevin Richards as executor. The will had originally nominated the decedent's brother, Kevin Kiernan, who declined to serve due to medical reasons. Mr. Wilson stated that under the terms of the trust, the successor trustee was to be appointed by the trust advisor. Although the trust declaration initially required appointment of a professional fiduciary, a later section authorized the trust advisor to amend the trust to correct ambiguities. Mr. Wilson asked the Court to deem Kathleen Hambly's appointment of Kevin Richards as an amendment under that provision.

The Court agreed that the trust contained an ambiguity and accepted the appointment of Kevin Richards as an amendment to the trust.

The Court found that the presumption of revocation under Probate Code Section 6124 had been overcome and granted the petition. There being no objection, the lost will dated December 17, 2010, is admitted to probate. Kevin R. Richards is appointed as the Executor with full authority. Bond is set at $20,000. Order for the same signed in open Court. A status hearing for filing of a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration is set for August 18, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. If either petition is filed two weeks prior, no appearance is required on that date. Isidoor Bornstein is appointed probate referee.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
